finding_results.notes: The snapshot's polished admonition ('If you report, make sure it's because you need to. Not because you're angry.') was already replaced in the current chapter with operational dialogue grounded in Harry's ledger register: 'Street exposure is his. Paper exposure is yours.' (line 271), 'That doesn't make him right about the deal.' (line 275), 'If the count moves, you wake me first. If I can't stop it, you do what you threatened.' (line 281), and 'Carmen, we're already in it.' (line 289). These lines deliver organizer-accountant logic — exposure categories, count triggers, conditional instructions — not mentor-figure wisdom. Additionally corrected two Agnes contractions in the aftermath ('didn't' to 'did not' at line 215, 'won't' to 'will not' at line 219) to restore her established low-contraction register, which she maintains consistently throughout the rest of the chapter.

Added two separate Carmen-internal beats before the legal-calculation turn: Earl's verdict now hits her as bodily/self-assessing pressure, and Harry's ruling now forces her to recognize that silence would implicate her before she issues the ultimatum.

pass_summaries.summary: Chapter 13 now lands the five-way moral split with distinct voices, turns the false-note aftermath into concrete operational consequence, and meets the 4000-word floor.

All other dialogue voices verified against style bible: Carmen's formal stress-register, Marcus's high-contraction casual speech, Earl's terse declarations, and Lola's winding self-corrective cadence are intact. No example lines from the style bible appear in the chapter. Continuity checked against the sheet — ages, room numbers, financial figures, and timeline all consistent.

Re-centered the late confrontation in Carmen's close-third interior without changing chapter architecture or outcome; the scene now reaches her ultimatum through embodied perception and self-assessment instead of extended external-only coverage.
